name: Butler Gulch — Butler Bowl
typical_duration_note: Single lap car-to-car. Multiple laps common — add ~1.5 hrs per additional lap.
gear_notes: Standard beacon, probe, shovel. Wind can strip snow above treeline — arrive early after storms.
description: The most beginner-friendly backcountry zone near Denver. Wide open west-facing bowl with forgiving terrain and a mellow, well-traveled skin track. Gets hammered by wind above treeline so powder windows are short after storms.

name: Butler Gulch — Point 12,085 Glades
typical_duration_note: Single lap car-to-car. Multiple laps common — add ~1.5 hrs per additional lap.
gear_notes: Standard beacon, probe, shovel. Requires traversing Butler Bowl to access — read avy conditions carefully on the open traverse.
description: A small NE-facing bowl and glade dropping off Point 12,085 into tight trees. A natural step up from Butler Bowl for skiers building confidence. More sheltered aspect means better snow preservation than the main bowl.

name: Butler Gulch — East Chutes (Hourglass)
typical_duration_note: Single lap car-to-car. Approach traverse adds time — factor in careful route-finding.
gear_notes: Standard beacon, probe, shovel. Significant avalanche exposure on traverse from Central Gully to East Chutes — travel one at a time. Central Gully is slide-prone and north-facing.
description: The longest and most committing lines in Butler Gulch. Steep west-facing chutes and glades funnel into tight tree gullies back to the skin track. Meaningful avy exposure on the approach traverse makes terrain awareness essential.

name: Jones Pass South — Jones Pass Bowl
typical_duration_note: Single lap car-to-car. Snowmobile-packed skin track typically speeds up approach.
gear_notes: Standard beacon, probe, shovel. Snowmobiles and snowcat operations active in this zone — shared motorized terrain.
description: Expansive north-facing bowl with numerous moderate options at and below treeline. Skin track typically pre-packed by snowmobile and snowcat traffic. Good early and late season option given north aspect and elevation.

name: Jones Pass — Point 12,118 Zone
typical_duration_note: Single lap car-to-car. Multiple aspect options allow for efficient lapping.
gear_notes: Standard beacon, probe, shovel. Solar Bowl gets significant sun — watch for slab formation on persistent weak layers. NE chutes hold cold smoke well.
description: A varied zone with options for intermediate and advanced skiers. SE and NE-facing glades, chutes, and Solar Bowl fan out from the base of Point 12,118. Better snow preservation on NE aspects than the main Jones Pass South bowl.

name: Jones Pass — West Fork Bowl (Peak 12,673)
typical_duration_note: Single lap car-to-car. Longest approach in the Jones Pass complex — plan for a full day.
gear_notes: Standard beacon, probe, shovel. Peak 12,666 chutes are the steepest lines — confident skiing in variable conditions required. Vasquez Saddle offers a lower-angle alternative within the same approach.
description: The biggest and most committing objective in the Jones Pass complex. A 2,200-foot approach leads to east-facing bowls and steep chutes off Peaks 12,673 and 12,666. Vasquez Saddle offers a lower-angle alternative for those wanting the approach without the consequence.
